authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 240.15c3-3 - Customer Protection; Reserves and Custody of Securities
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Rule 15c3-3 is the primary U.S. custody-control rule for safeguarding customer funds and securities, possession or control treatment, reserve implications, and good-control-location discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer asset protection and prerequisite control review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains what the lane can treat as held safely, releasable, or complete when control-location or safeguarding evidence is missing or contradictory.

authoritative_sources.title: Protection of Customer Assets
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA annual oversight guidance is authoritative examinations-driven operating guidance for customer-asset protection, possession-or-control deficits, reserve quality, and reconciliation failures observed in market practice.
used_for: escalation posture for customer-asset protection issues
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic failure handling and refusal triggers so custody exceptions do not get normalized into routine back-office noise.

authoritative_sources.title: Custody Service Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DTC Custody Service Guide is an official market-infrastructure procedure source for deposits, withdrawals, transfers, restricted deposits, and related status progression used by custody operators.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Custody receipt, delivery, and safekeeping progression
used_for: deposit, withdrawal, and transfer workflow states
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains lifecycle states and evidence expectations for custody movements so pending or rejected activity is not mistaken for completed safekeeping work.

authoritative_sources.title: ALERT - SSI maintenance, communication and automation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC ALERT is an authoritative public workflow reference for standing settlement instruction ownership, communication, and automation across custodians and market participants.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines why stale or conflicting SSI data is a custody blocker rather than a minor data-cleanup task.

authoritative_sources.title: Settlement Instruction Manager
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC SIM is an authoritative workflow reference for instruction lifecycle history, cancellation, release, and settlement-message-driven exception handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Instruction lifecycle progression and exception tracking
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to use realistic instruction states and to distinguish in-flight, cancelled, released, and exception statuses instead of generic processing language.

authoritative_sources.title: DTC Settlement Service Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DTC Settlement Service Guide is an official procedure source for deliveries, RAD review, reclaims, pledges, segregations, and pending-transaction behavior that frequently intersects with custody operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Depository settlement dependencies, pledges, and pending states
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains what custody can call complete when depository, pledge, or settlement states remain open or contradictory.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 4511 - General Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 4511 is a core books-and-records baseline governing preservation and retrievability of evidence needed to support custody execution, exception notes, and closure claims.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines why the lane must produce completion outputs only when the underlying custody, depository, and ledger records are preserved and retrievable.

authoritative_sources.title: SEA Rule 17a-3 and Related Interpretations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA's Rule 17a-3 interpretations are authoritative for the records firms must make current around receipts and deliveries, fails, securities positions, and related custody activity.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines the named artifacts the lane must request before asserting record truth, exception closure, or completed custody movement.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and custody prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate account, asset, market, depository, SSI, control-location, authorization, and approval context before custody progression.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: SSI and instruction governance
workflow_stages.description: Move the case through pending SSI review, instruction accepted, instruction amended, instruction released, or instruction cancelled states using named instruction and status records.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Custody movement, safekeeping, and control-location progression
workflow_stages.description: Track deposit, withdrawal, transfer, pledge, segregation, pending depository, and settled states without narrating unresolved custody movement as completed.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and blocked-state routing
workflow_stages.description: Summarize rejected instructions, control-location deficits, custody breaks, restricted-position issues, and conflicting records with explicit blocker ownership and no unsupported clearance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Produce completion outputs only when authoritative custody, depository, position, cash, and ledger records support closure.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Custody account master, participant identifier, safekeeping account, and control-location records
Standing settlement instructions, ALERT or equivalent SSI records, instruction release or cancellation history, and custodian-account details
Deposit, withdrawal, transfer, restricted-deposit, pledge, segregation, and release instruction records with timestamps and approvals
Custodian, sub-custodian, depository, transfer-agent, or SWIFT MT54x status messages and exception details
Position, stock-record, possession-or-control, cash, nostro, suspense, and general-ledger records tied to the custody event
Case-management notes, escalation artifacts, discrepancy reports, and client or counterparty communication records
systems_of_record: SSI master and settlement-instruction systems such as ALERT, SIM, or tenant equivalents
Custodian, sub-custodian, depository, and transfer-agent processing platforms
Position, stock-record, cash, nostro, general-ledger, and reconciliation systems
Compliance, sanctions, approval, and escalation workflow systems

allowed: Determine whether available records support progression, hold, rework, or completion within custody execution workflows.
Produce execution summaries, completion evidence, exception notes, and escalation packages tied to named custody instructions, SSI records, custodian or depository statuses, and ledger support.
Identify when custody blockers require the next operational owner, approver, or adjacent specialist.
escalate: A customer-asset-protection, sanctions, legal, compliance, treasury, collateral, or client-impact indicator changes the risk posture.
A legal, policy, depository, account-structure, or asset-form interpretation materially changes whether the request may proceed.
Custody, depository, control-location, position, cash, or ledger records conflict and need human review or adjacent-owner resolution.
refuse: The request asks for investment advice, trade-booking ownership, collateral approval, treasury funding approval, legal interpretation, sanctions disposition, or final compliance signoff outside the lane.
The request asks to release a hold, waive a deficit, approve a pledge or restriction change, or mark work complete without authoritative evidence.
Required tenant facts, custody context, or system-of-record evidence are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing a custody movement without an authoritative instruction, SSI source, or control-location record.
Treating pending, held, pledged, segregated, partial, or rejected custody and depository states as completed work.
Releasing a restriction, pledge, or custody hold without the documented approval owner and supporting evidence.
Collapsing custodian, depository, and internal books-and-records states into one narrative state when they materially conflict.
Assuming all custody work follows the same DTC-centered or broker-dealer workflow without verifying asset form, jurisdiction, and market context.
Drifting into collateral approval, treasury funding, corporate actions, adviser-custody interpretation, or legal or compliance authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-U.S., non-DTC, and local-market custody procedures
unresolved_ambiguities.details: Local market rules, foreign sub-custodian practices, and CSD-specific message flows may impose extra prerequisites that are not fully covered by the U.S.-centric public baseline used in this pack.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack requires tenant-supplied market and depository context before progression and routes to a more specialized lane when the local market rule set changes the outcome.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Investment-adviser custody interpretation versus broker-dealer custody operations
unresolved_ambiguities.details: Asset-management requests may involve adviser-custody obligations or qualified-custodian interpretations that differ from broker-dealer customer-protection controls.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack treats adviser-custody interpretation as out of scope and routes to compliance or legal specialists when the governing regime is not explicit.
